name: ETH Range Failure Reacceptance v1
edge_mechanism: range-failure reversal after multi-bar breakout loses acceptance and re-enters balance
thesis: ETH 1h is the best primary lane for testing whether a multi-bar break from balance that fails to gain acceptance can be faded once price re-enters value. The edge is not blind fade-every-breakout behavior; it is selective range-failure reacceptance after the market proves the breakout was rejected.
invalidation_condition: Abort if failed-break reversals remain too sparse, if reacceptance back into balance does not travel enough after costs, or if breakouts continue rather than failing.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price extends out of balance for more than one bar, loses acceptance, re-enters value, and then rotates back through the prior range far enough for a tradable reversal leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Choppy intraday markets are full of false acceptance attempts. Waiting for explicit re-entry into value should capture the repair move while avoiding the first countertrend stab.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 1h is the best lane for this because failed range breaks are visible, dense enough to test, and cleaner than on thinner alts.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Some breaks may re-enter value but not travel far enough before stalling again, leaving the reversal too weak after fees.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries trigger with reasonable density but travel is too shallow, reduce target ambition before tightening the failure conditions.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A workable number of failed-break reversals with front-loaded exits doing most of the expectancy.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 1h plus one cross-lane show credible density and positive post-cost rotation after failed acceptance.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, low-trade mirage, or reacceptance moves that repeatedly fail to rotate back through value.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; breakout extension, reacceptance, and exhaustion are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad failure-and-return grammar rather than exact candle perfection

validation_basket.why_included: Best clean lane for failed-range reacceptance in the live basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: Reacceptance reversals are frequent enough and travel back through balance after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Failed breaks remain sparse or do not rotate far enough.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Clear failed breakouts that revert back through range value.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same failure-and-reacceptance mechanism survives on the similar major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC preserves the same reversal shape with acceptable density.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Similar but slightly cleaner range-failure reversals.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same grammar generalizes to a slower adjacent lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: 4h preserves the mechanism without collapsing trade count.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner failed-break reversals than 1h.

long: Exit remaining 20% on close through EMA_55 or 4 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on close through EMA_55 or 4 bars
